Well, I'm super, super extra excited to be here because usually when I speak, I come from the MLM industry and I speak a lot and usually it's to students. So briefly my story. I'm 31. I'm 31. I feel old now because I used to tell a story. I'm like, I'm 25, made my first million. I'm 31. But I've been in this industry since like eighth grade. I did my first chain letter when I was in eighth grade. I had to have my mom sign papers so I could join Herbalife at 14 and then Amway at 15. I was a junkie, I guess. Anyone else junkies here? There's rehab for it. But like Don the Pre, Brad Richdale, everything, right? So fast forward like, you know, I remember being 23 living in my parents' basement because I just, I never gave up on that dream, I guess. I just couldn't settle for anything else. But I tried everything and I was doing MLM and all these types of different things. And I was just, it is just this place of desperation, right? I'm going to tell my story. It's usually 10 minutes, but after, is Michael Hague here? Where's he at? So I turned my story to about an hour and a half to go through. So, no, but I just remember being super depressed and, you know, anyone do MLM here before it started? So, I mean, that's where I got started. So my big breakthrough came, you know, I joined a direct sales company and it was like $3,895 to join. I spent like three months saving up for it. That was a ton of money. I had like $200 left over for traffic, right? So I tried to make it work, normal methods, things like that. And I couldn't make it work. So I started to, I had this idea in my head. I was like, you know what, I'm going to figure this out. So I started calling all the top people in the industry to pretend to be a prospect, right? My thinking was, you know what, if I call all of these people, pretend to be a prospect, I can figure out what they're doing. So like, Dagan's a big guy, I call him like, hey, you know, and your opportunities like $3,895 to join, you know, if I do that, I have like $200 left over to spend, you know, would that put me in profit? I went to the next guru and everything wrote down all this stuff. And I was more depressed than ever because I tried all those things, right? So I just remember my moment, my epiphany moment was, I just remember being in bed late at night, just super depressed, super crying. I just had this like, like this just light bulb moment, you know, like the Bugs Bunny when the light bulb was over the head, like it was just one of those. It was just this huge like period of just utter desperation, like I got it. And I got two things and I implemented them. And my first month I did four sales and I made 12 grand. It's just pretty much slowly change the perception of being sold to them, feeling like they have to prove themselves to you. And it's their decision to buy. This is my favorite quote. I love this. If you need heart surgery, you aren't gonna go with a doctor and get you in right away, right? And that's kind of something you wanna think about as you're doing this. If you think about it, you know, you have a lot of customers in your business. And for some of them, it is as big as heart surgery. So people who like, can make money niche, right? They have to make a lot of money. They're like super, super desperate, right? But when you're going to the doctor, you're gonna go to the one who's just like, yeah, we can get you in right away, right? Doesn't make sense. And I know when you guys would consult with like chiropractors and things like that, and it's a new business, you know, they'd go two weeks without a phone call. They get their first phone call in and they put them on hold right away, right? And then, you know, they pick up the phone. Yes, can I help you? We'd like to, do you have any appointments for the doctor? Man, she was slammed. Let me see, right? So they have the phone, they're going through the notebook, going through the fake scheduling. You know, it's like Monday, right? Like, maybe Tuesday next week, 4.15, how does that sound, right? Well, I'll take it, right? Who, if you needed heart surgery, who would you wanna go see? The one you can get in right away or the one that makes you wait for it, right? So we kinda think about that. When we talk, at the end of this, this whole quote will make sense. So there's a few different steps that we have. The first tweak that I did, and some of you guys already do this, is pretty easy, having them apply. It's application and a strategy session. So having them apply for what you're selling, and the number two is at the end, that's the close. I don't call it a close, I call it a strategy session. So if you're the God, if you're the Godfather, and they're talking to you, it's not a close where you're convincing them where this is what you need, it's a strategy session. I forget who said this, but prescription without diagnosis is malpractice. And it's the same thing here, right? So on a strategy session, you're the doctor, right? And you're kinda listening to where they are in their business and things like that, giving them tips and everything of that. You're not in the phone being like, how much money do you have? This is the features and benefits of what we have. So those are the two big tweaks. So the first was the application process. It's just super psychological. It puts them in a place where they're proving themselves to you, right? And you use wording on there, this doesn't guarantee you a spot. You're applying to be a part of what we have. You're applying to be a partner. You're applying to get coaching from us. This isn't something that it's gonna be a high ticket program and we're gonna sell as many as we can. So scarcity is a huge, huge key factor. Does that make sense? And a lot of times people ask, well how much, do you charge for the application? Well it depends. If you're someone who's doing low volume, start with the free application, just to get the ball rolling. If you're someone who's high volume, start charging more. Like I know Frank Kern, he just went to selling high ticket programs and what he does, he charges a thousand dollar deposit. And if you're not accepted, you get refunded, your thousand dollar deposit. The way we would do it, I mean all of our traffic was back in the day with social media, right? So I'm telling you my evolution as I learned this. And they go through our main sales letter and the videos stuff were there and it would just be an application. It would just be an application. I don't know traffic how it is now in terms of cold versus warm and things like that. I haven't done any tests lately with traffic and stuff like that, but I think either way. But the question is, are you gonna sell a high ticket right away or do you have smaller products that they buy anyways or beforehand? I'm starting to figure out how small is it that it's about getting that initial from an offer buyer. Yeah, I mean a lot of you have like lower tickets and they go higher and higher and higher. One of the things that I like to do, since I'm like in the make money niches, you know, if you are selected as a partner, you know, what's your budget for traffic? Right? Like for our partner program, we're going to be priced up front. Right? It's like, we want to partner with you, help out your funnel. If you send traffic to it, I want a percentage of the profit, right? So I pitched that and I say, you know what? But I'm not going to spend the money on traffic, you know, things like that. So what's your budget for traffic? A lot of times they'd actually put their real budget instead of saying, you know, I only have a few grand. If they really have a lot of money to spend on traffic, they put like 10 grand or something. So you'll get the people and you'll find out, you know, who has money, who doesn't. So the people who have, you know, only $500, you know, you'll have someone close them on like a lower ticket product or, you know, something like that. But the ones that have a higher, you keep putting them through this whole process. Does that make sense? The second, the second I discovered this by accident, my girlfriend needed a job at the time. So what I had her do was call my leads and set up appointments, right? Just appointment setting. And the conversion's more than doubled. Just by having an assistant doing nothing else. I didn't tell her what to say, what to do. Just by having an assistant, the sales doubled and cut my time by 80%. Is there a script involved or is it just kind of just wins it each time? Well, as we go, I'll tell you kind of stuff we, you know, some of the stuff we coached around, things like that. But we do have a script, but it's only for the mastermind members, so. That's right. But there's a lot of reasons why it works. There's a ton of reasons why it works. And this whole funnel is like for me closing. I know a lot of you guys use phone rooms. I know a lot of you guys don't want to close by yourself. But the beautiful thing is, you know, if you're someone who's afraid to be on the phone or don't like closing, this is the most fun you'll ever have. So by the time they get to you, they are so nervous to talk to you. So it's like meeting your best friend for the first time. By the time they get to the end of this. So, you know, having the assistant tells them you're a big deal, right? You know, and then as we tweaked it, we started working on things to really have the assistant build us up, right? You know, as a rock star. And we got down to a science, but I used to tell our students to get a fake assistant. Go get a fake email at least, like Sandra275 at Gmail and email the prospect as the assistant, if you couldn't do that, right? To kind of set things up. And that worked too. So, we have a few things. So after the application, so the funnel went to, so we started doing having the application, the assistant would call and set up the appointment and then I would talk to them. That's how it went. And then we have a couple more steps that we started adding. So we have the first call. So she kind of calls, like, you know what? I've pre-approved your application. I've personally pre-approved. And they want to kind of build you up more on the phone. One of the things I like to do is have them find a fault in one of the questions. And I noticed you put that as one of your fears, you know? So I just wanted to ask you more about that. You know, that sort of thing, right? It puts them on the spot, right? I see you smiling like you're starting to get that. Is this good stuff? I mean, you guys? So then what I do next is I give them homework to do. And so it's like the assistants on the phone say, okay, before I get you on the phone with Jimmy, I have some homework for you to do. And the faster you get this done, the faster I can set up the appointment with you and Jimmy. The homework was key for us. Cause I get on the phone with these guys. And a lot of times, you know, I noticed I was answering the same questions beforehand. All the time. That was a problem. I noticed some of these people still, I wasn't built up enough or they didn't know who I was. I went by the time that I talked to them, they have a Jimmy Davis poster on their wall. And they have like a religious ceremony like every week. So this is all this process is doing. So I would get pissed off if I didn't close someone, you know? Cause I believe so much in our product. And he's like the nicest guy in the world. They're scared to death. They get to him. It's just like, magnifold the emotion. He's right there. It's like, I wish I had more money to give you, right? So right now the timeline is, after they fill out the application, the assistant is usually like one or two days, like the first call in the appointment. And that's what we get to next, like set up the appointment. So we go to the homework page, right? This is where I put a bunch of stuff on a page. I get the FAQs out of the way, right? They're committed to buying something, even if they didn't buy something. The more committed you have them do, the better. So we started giving them a homework page. So like 3.15, 3.45, instead of like 3.30 and 4, it's kind of an insurance, people do insurance sales, things like that, instead of the appointments. It makes you sound super busy if you do like 4.15, 5.15, you know, like 4.45. Doesn't like crease versions like crazy, but it's just kind of a little tip kind of thing. Yeah, so what level you're gonna try to take them to after that one. Yeah, and the way I look at it too, is I look at it like a doctor's office, right? If you're a doctor, you don't prescribe one thing for everyone. It's not like, oh you have knee pain, Viagra. You know, that works for everything. But you know, there's different things. And so as a strategy, if you have other products and services, not just one, I mean you can have them apply for like, you know, publishing thing or whatever. I have a couple more things that, I have a couple really cool things to show. The reverse close is one thing I do, digging like this. Instead of me closing over the phone or, you know, asking for the money there. One thing that I found worked for me and some of you might think goes against like intuitively, get the money right there. But I started trying this reverse close where when I'm talking to them, I, you know, I'm like talking to Deygan. And it's like, I think you'd be perfect for it. But here's the thing, I have to talk to my partner. As you know, we're only taking 20 clients on. And this isn't my decision. If it was my decision, I'd say yes, I like you. Right, I'd like you. But I have to talk to my partner. I have to talk to my partner. But here's the thing, we go through each application. I'm gonna go through my partner with each application. It takes a half hour to go through each one. So I need to know that if you are accepted, you will be able to pay for the whole thing. Cause I'm not gonna go waste a half hour on my partner's time and you can't do it. So I need to know if, you know, if you are accepting, and I'm not saying you are. So I'm not saying you are. So don't, you know, celebrate yet. But if you are accepted, would you be willing to, are you ready to go? Right, and they said, of course. Like yes, absolutely. And like I said, it's yes for me. But I got a good feeling about you, you gotta go through it. Stay by your phone and we'll be in touch. Kind of thing, right? System will call you back.
